mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or Rank IC > 0.05 in opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high percentiles of RSAFS and HOUST yield negative forward returns with statistical significance (p < 0.05) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers immediate positive repricing of equity earnings over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 70% of cases across historical samples.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns with a Rank IC below -0.04 over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate profit margins, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across all tested macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.3 percent signals weakening manufacturing demand and inventory drawdowns, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines above the threshold are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances over a 10-year backtest.
